title: Fish Szechuan Sauce

ingredients.title: For the Fish:
items: 500g white fish fillets (cod, seabass, or tilapia), skinless

ingredients.title: For the Szechuan Sauce:
items: 5-6 dried red chilies (adjust to heat preference), cut into halves
1 tsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tsp water (slurry, optional for thicker sauce)

instruction.title: Prepare the Fish
steps: Pat fish fillets dry. Season lightly with salt, white pepper, and Shaoxing wine.
Dust each fillet evenly with cornstarch.
Heat oil in a non-stick or stainless steel pan over medium-high heat.
Pan-fry fish until golden and crisp on both sides (about 2–3 minutes per side, depending on thickness). Remove and set aside on a warm plate.

instruction.title: Make the Szechuan Sauce
steps: In a wok or skillet, heat 2 tbsp oil over medium heat.
Add dried chilies and Szechuan peppercorns; stir-fry briefly until fragrant (don’t burn, about 30 seconds).
Add minced garlic and ginger; sauté until aromatic.
Stir in Doubanjiang (chili bean paste), cook 20–30 seconds to release its flavor.
Add soy sauce, black vinegar, sugar, and stock; bring to a simmer.
Add scallions and cook for another 1–2 minutes.
If you want a thicker sauce, stir in the cornstarch slurry and cook until glossy.
Finish with a drizzle of sesame oil for aroma.

instruction.title: Combine & Serve
steps: Plate the fish fillets and spoon the hot Szechuan sauce generously over the top.
Garnish with fresh cilantro, sliced scallions, or toasted sesame seeds.
Serve immediately with steamed jasmine rice or stir-fried vegetables.

chefTips: Crisp Fish: Ensure the fish is patted dry and the pan is hot enough before frying. This keeps the fish tender inside but crisp outside.
Flavor Balance: Doubanjiang is salty and spicy; taste and adjust soy or sugar to achieve a balanced heat and umami.
Optional Aromatics: For a richer aroma, add a few thin slices of red bell pepper or carrot for color and crunch.
Szechuan Peppercorns: Lightly toast them to release the numbing aroma, but don’t over-toast—they’ll become bitter.
